name: Pork Chops Calvados
recipeInstructions:
1. In an oven-safe skillet, melt the butter over medium heat.
2. Saute' the pork chops until browned on all sides.
3. Sprinkle the chops with salt and pepper, then cover and bake in a 350-degree oven for 45 minutes.
4. Combine the grated Gruyere cheese, Dijon mustard, and 4 tablespoons of heavy cream in a bowl. Spread the mixture evenly over the pork chops.
5. Place the skillet under the broiler and broil until the cheese is lightly browned (5-6 inches from heat). Remove immediately.
6. Transfer the pork chops to a serving platter and keep warm. Reserve all drippings in the skillet.
7. Add the Calvados brandy to the drippings, then cook over high heat, deglazing the skillet by scraping particles clinging to the bottom.
8. Reduce the heat to medium-low and add the remaining heavy cream. Cook, stirring constantly until slightly thickened.
9. Pour the sauce over the pork chops and sprinkle with paprika and parsley.
